scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within specialized research tooling scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: TypeError (list & list) fixed in v4: set() conversion applied in check_coauthorship_conflict(). NameError in check_institutional_conflict() fixed: 'reviewer' replaced with reviewer_name parameter. Collaboration window stub still documented as known limitation.

reliability.note: Both runtime bugs fixed. CSV error handling now uses try/except with clear FileNotFoundError and generic Exception messages. Script runs cleanly on canonical input without fallback required.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 145 lines — concise. Script is minimal at 172 lines. Very low token cost.

agent_usability.note: Script now executes correctly for coauthorship detection without requiring Claude Direct fallback. Institutional conflict path still documented as Claude Direct but script method is now also fixed. Error handling rules updated to reflect fixed state.

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable for journal submission and editorial use cases. Scope boundaries are well-defined.

security.note: No credential handling. Demo data hardcoded in script is acceptable for testing. No injection vectors. CSV parsing uses DictReader safely.

maintainability.note: Clean single-file script. Both bugs fixed. Known limitations documented. CSV error handling added. Collaboration window stub documented.

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise. Escape hatches present. Institutional conflict path documented. Script now executes correctly for primary use case. Collaboration window stub still unimplemented — documented gap.

inputs.label: Check coauthorship conflicts for Smith/Jones/Lee vs Brown/Davis/Wilson
inputs.note: Script executes without error. TypeError fix confirmed: set() conversion in check_coauthorship_conflict() resolves list & list intersection. Correctly identifies 3 conflicts: Brown-Smith (paper1), Wilson-Smith (paper2), Wilson-Jones (paper2).

assertions.text: Script executes without runtime error on canonical input
assertions.note: TypeError fix confirmed — set() conversion applied at line 48
3 conflicts correctly identified: Brown-Smith, Wilson-Smith, Wilson-Jones

assertions.text: Output includes Accept/Recuse recommendation for each reviewer
assertions.note: Recommendations section present in report output

assertions.text: Output stays within conflict-of-interest checking scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Check conflicts using a custom CSV publication file
inputs.note: CSV-based conflict detection works correctly. CSV error handling confirmed: FileNotFoundError produces clear error message and exits cleanly. Valid CSV correctly loads and detects conflicts.

assertions.text: Output loads CSV correctly and uses it instead of demo data
assertions.note: DictReader loads author and paper_id columns correctly

assertions.text: Output correctly identifies conflicts from CSV records
assertions.note: Brown-Smith conflict from paper1 correctly detected

assertions.text: CSV error handling produces clear message on missing file
assertions.note: FileNotFoundError caught and reported: 'Error: CSV file not found'

assertions.text: Output does not exceed stated scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

inputs.label: Check institutional conflict between reviewer and author at same institution
inputs.note: NameError fix confirmed: check_institutional_conflict() now uses reviewer_name parameter correctly. Script method works. Claude Direct path also documented as alternative. Both paths produce correct institutional conflict detection.

assertions.note: NameError fix confirmed — reviewer_name parameter used correctly at line 68

assertions.text: Institutional conflict is detected when reviewer and author share the same institution
assertions.note: Case-insensitive comparison correctly flags shared institution

assertions.text: Output reports institutional conflict type separately from coauthorship
Output does not exceed stated scope
assertions.note: Output remained within the skill's stated operational scope.

key_strengths: TypeError (list & list) fixed: set() conversion in check_coauthorship_conflict() enables correct script execution on canonical input
NameError in check_institutional_conflict() fixed: reviewer_name parameter now used correctly, enabling script-level institutional conflict detection
CSV error handling added: FileNotFoundError and generic exceptions caught with clear error messages
Collaboration window stub clearly documented as known limitation with no silent failure

recommendations.title: Collaboration window check is an unimplemented stub
recommendations.problem: check_collaboration_conflict() always returns an empty list. This feature is implied by the skill's description but silently does nothing.
recommendations.root_cause: The method was scaffolded but not implemented; no publication date data is loaded from the CSV.
recommendations.fix: Either implement date-based collaboration detection using the CSV (add a 'year' column to the format), or remove the method and update SKILL.md to state that only coauthorship and institutional conflicts are checked.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md Known Limitations note still references NameError as active bug
recommendations.problem: The Known Limitations note in SKILL.md still says 'check_institutional_conflict() has a NameError bug at line 59' — this is now fixed in v4 and the note is outdated.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md was not updated to reflect the v4 script fix.
recommendations.fix: Update the Known Limitations note to remove the NameError reference. Update the Institutional Conflict section to note that the script method is now functional, while the Claude Direct path remains available as an alternative.
